_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-37R Nitroglycerin (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-37R: Nitroglycerin — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) — chest pain/ischemic symptoms
Hypertensive emergency with acute pulmonary edema (per applicable protocol)
contraindications: Use of erectile dysfunction (ED) medications within ≤48 hours (sildenafil/Viagra, tadalafil/Cialis, vardenafil/Levitra) — severe hypotension risk
Use of pulmonary hypertension medications within ≤48 hours (riociguat, sildenafil)
With IV access established: SBP <100 mmHg
Inferior STEMI with suspected RV involvement: extreme caution — may cause severe hypotension (contact online MC)
precautions: Check BP before each dose — DO NOT give if pressure below thresholds
Always ask about ED/PH medications before administration
May cause significant headache (vasodilation effect — expected)
Without IV access: maximum 3 doses total; with IV access: may repeat q3-5 minutes
expected_effects: Coronary vasodilation and preload reduction → decreased myocardial oxygen demand
Decreased venous return and preload (beneficial in pulmonary edema)
notes: Standard dose: 0.4 mg sublingual tablet or spray
Without IV access: max 3 doses total; check BP before each dose; SBP must be ≥120
With IV access established: may repeat q3-5 minutes; SBP must be ≥100
Always ask: 'Have you taken Viagra, Cialis, Levitra or similar medication in the last 48 hours?' before giving
Inferior STEMI: check right-sided leads or contact online MC before giving — RV infarct involvement

dosing.dose: 0.4 mg SL (one tablet or one spray)
dosing.notes: Without IV: max 3 doses; SBP ≥120 before each dose. With IV: repeat q3-5 min; SBP ≥100. Ask about ED/PH medications before giving
